trust_explanation.summary: Your automation system is currently producing signals that cannot be relied on for release decisions, creating risk of shipping defects or blocking valid releases.
drivers: State behavior is inconsistent across executions, making test outcomes unreliable
Timing-sensitive failures are introducing false positives and false negatives in CI
Test data does not reflect production conditions, reducing confidence in release readiness

issues.detail: State setup is not fully deterministic across flows.
issues.risk: Test results can pass or fail depending on execution order, creating false release confidence.

issues.detail: Execution depends on timing-sensitive UI behavior.
issues.risk: Timing-sensitive failures make CI signal inconsistent and reduce trust in failures and passes.

issues.detail: Test data does not fully reflect release-like conditions.
issues.risk: Coverage may look healthy while production-critical states remain untested.

cost_estimation.note: Fix state and timing instability across core flows (~6–10 engineering hours).
Improve diagnosability, reduce repeat failure classes, and tighten data control (~14–20 hours).
Add release gating, stronger confidence controls, and structured failure triage (~28–36 hours).

recommended_path: Stabilize state setup across test flows (Sprint 5)
Remove timing dependencies and improve determinism (Sprint 6)
Introduce release gating and failure classification (Sprint 6–7)
next_step_cta: We can implement these fixes and move your framework to production-ready within 2–3 sprints. Reply to your report email to get started.
